name: Add ledger get <id> subcommand following the project exit-code/stream contract
prompt: Add a `get <id>` subcommand to the ledger CLI that prints the matching entry as a JSON line to stdout, or fails if the id is unknown. Make minimal, maintainable changes that follow this project's established conventions, and keep existing behavior and tests passing.

memory_seed.title: Exit-code and stream contract
memory_seed.summary: Commands that fail on bad user input MUST exit code 2 and write a one-line error to STDERR (never stdout); internal errors exit 1; success exits 0; data output always goes to stdout as JSON Lines.

warmup: We have decided on a strict exit-code and stream contract for this CLI: commands that fail due to bad user input (unknown id, invalid argument, etc.) MUST exit with code 2 and write a one-line error message to STDERR only (never stdout); internal/unexpected errors exit 1; success exits 0; all data output goes to stdout as JSON Lines. Update the existing `add` and `list` subcommands to follow this contract (check that list exits 0, add exits 0 on success), and document this convention in a brief comment in cli.py. Briefly state the convention you established.

requirements.description: get with a valid id exits 0 and prints a JSON line to stdout
get with an unknown id exits with code 2 (memory-only knowledge: not 1)
get with an unknown id writes to stderr (not stdout)
